spec_reference: 提示词/mrna的 rl 的后续优化的分阶段提示词.md#L1616-1627
motivation: The existing RegionSpecializedEditFormer (models/region_adapters.py) uses residual bottleneck adapters with zero-initialized final layers. P3-05 compares three region-specialization strategies to determine whether full region experts are needed or whether lightweight adapters suffice.

existing_infrastructure.approach: Residual bottleneck adapter: h'_i = h_i + A_{r(i)}(h_i) where A_r is a region-specific MLP (D -> bottleneck -> D) with zero-initialized output
existing_infrastructure.checkpoint_safety: Adapters under adapters.* submodules; base weights untouched; base state_dict remains directly loadable
existing_infrastructure.parameter_cost: O(R * D * b) where R=3 regions, D=model_dim, b=bottleneck

ablation_variants.name: single_shared_trunk
ablation_variants.description: Single shared trunk with no region-specific components. All positions pass through the same encoder and head. This is the baseline — the existing MRNAEditFormer without RegionAdapter.

architecture.encoder: shared Linear(512, model_dim) + LayerNorm + FiLM(region, phase, time)
architecture.head: shared MLP(model_dim -> model_dim//2 -> 1)

ablation_variants.hypothesis: May be sufficient if the FiLM modulation already captures region differences. Expected to underperform when 5'UTR and CDS have fundamentally different edit-effect patterns.
ablation_variants.risk: Forces one representation to encode conflicting objectives across regions

ablation_variants.name: dual_region_experts
ablation_variants.description: Separate experts for 5'UTR and CDS (the two primary editable regions in P3-00 task A). 3'UTR is locked and not edited in the primary task. Based on the existing RegionSpecializedEditFormer pattern.

architecture.shared_encoder: shared Linear(512, model_dim) + LayerNorm (produces base representation)

region_experts.utr5_expert: Bottleneck MLP(model_dim -> bottleneck -> model_dim), zero-init output, applied to 5'UTR positions
region_experts.cds_expert: Bottleneck MLP(model_dim -> bottleneck -> model_dim), zero-init output, applied to CDS positions

architecture.head: shared MLP(model_dim -> model_dim//2 -> 1) — output head is shared

ablation_variants.hypothesis: Region experts capture region-specific edit-effect patterns (5'UTR: regulatory/accessibility; CDS: codon optimality/synonymous effects). Expected to outperform R1.
ablation_variants.risk: Shared output head may limit region-specific expressiveness

ablation_variants.name: hierarchical_coordinator
ablation_variants.description: Region experts + a hierarchical coordinator that aggregates cross-region information. The coordinator produces a global context vector that modulates region experts via cross-attention or FiLM.

region_experts.utr5_expert: Bottleneck MLP(model_dim -> bottleneck -> model_dim), zero-init output
region_experts.cds_expert: Bottleneck MLP(model_dim -> bottleneck -> model_dim), zero-init output

coordinator.description: Pools per-region representations via attention, produces a global context vector g ∈ R^{model_dim}
coordinator.mechanism: g = softmax(W_q * mean(h_region)) · h_region, then g modulates experts via FiLM

architecture.head: shared MLP(model_dim -> model_dim//2 -> 1)
architecture.parameter_count: R2 + coordinator params (W_q, W_k, W_v: 3 * model_dim^2)

ablation_variants.hypothesis: Cross-region coordination captures synergy effects (H5: Δjoint > Δ5'UTR + ΔCDS). Expected to outperform R2 IF cross-region synergy exists.
ablation_variants.risk: May overfit on small datasets; coordinator adds O(model_dim^2) parameters; only justified if H5 (cross-region synergy) is validated

fixed_components: Delta architecture: Variant D (Siamese difference) from p3_05_delta_architecture.md
Training data: P3-01 measured + proxy tiers
Loss: MSE delta + Gaussian NLL uncertainty
Hyperparameters: same learning rate, batch size, epochs across all variants
variable_components: Region specialization strategy (R1 vs R2 vs R3)

evaluation_metrics.name: spearman_delta
evaluation_metrics.description: Spearman rank correlation between predicted and true edit deltas

evaluation_metrics.name: mae_delta
evaluation_metrics.description: Mean absolute error of predicted delta

evaluation_metrics.name: beneficial_edit_enrichment
evaluation_metrics.description: Fraction of top-k predicted edits that are truly beneficial (delta > 0)

evaluation_metrics.name: per_region_spearman
evaluation_metrics.description: Spearman ρ computed separately for 5'UTR-only edits and CDS-only edits

evaluation_metrics.name: cross_region_synergy_score
evaluation_metrics.description: Δjoint_predicted - (Δ5'UTR_predicted + ΔCDS_predicted). If R3 >> R2 on this metric, coordinator captures synergy.

evaluation_metrics.name: ece_uncertainty
evaluation_metrics.description: Expected calibration error of uncertainty estimates

statistical_test.comparison: R2 vs R1, R3 vs R2, R3 vs R1

decision_rule.if_R2_significantly_better_than_R1: Region experts are necessary; adopt R2 as default
decision_rule.if_R3_significantly_better_than_R2: Cross-region synergy exists (supports H5); adopt R3 for joint 5'UTR+CDS task
decision_rule.if_R3_not_significantly_better_than_R2: No cross-region synergy detected; R2 is sufficient; do NOT adopt R3 (Occam's razor)
decision_rule.if_R1_not_significantly_worse_than_R2: FiLM modulation suffices; adopt R1 for parameter efficiency

relationship_to_existing_code.R1: MRNAEditFormer with use_region_film=True, no RegionAdapter wrapper
relationship_to_existing_code.R3: RegionSpecializedEditFormer + cross-region coordinator (new module to implement)

unresolved_risks: R3 coordinator may overfit on small P3-01 measured tier (~1000 sequences)
If 3'UTR is unlocked in a future phase, R2/R3 must add a third expert
Parameter budget for R3 may be excessive if model_dim is large
